generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance OperationalLogSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Automated sensor capturing operational logs and event traces from physical system components for contextual data aggregation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OperationalLogDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalParameterSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Distributed sensor network capturing spatial-temporal environmental parameters (temperature, humidity, pressure) for system monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalParameterDataTransmitted
]

instance MachineStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-frequency sensor capturing machine state metrics (speed, torque, vibration) for real-time performance monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MachineStateDataTransmitted
]

instance ContextualDataSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor capturing contextual data traces including time-series data, event logs, and structured metadata from physical assets."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ContextualDataTracesTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance OperationalLogDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OperationalLogSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalParameterDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalParameterSensor
]

instance MachineStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MachineStateSensor
]

instance ContextualDataTracesTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ContextualDataSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLFunctionalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram representing functional architecture components and their interconnections at requirements level."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance SimulinkBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink model representing detailed behavioral dynamics and control logic for physical system components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngineEnabler
]

instance AADLComponentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "AADL model defining component interfaces, connectors, and composition for physical/technical system layer."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ArchitectureDescriptionEnabler
]

instance HistoricalOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated historical operational data traces including time-series sensor readings and event logs for analytics."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ContextualDataTracesTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAnalysisEnabler
]

instance RealTimeMachineStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time processed machine state data derived from high-frequency sensor inputs for monitoring and control."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MachineStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlSystemEnabler
]

instance EnvironmentalContextData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed environmental context data including temperature, humidity, and pressure parameters for system performance analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalParameterDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PerformanceAnalysisEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based modeling framework enabling functional/logical architecture representation and stakeholder integration."
    DTDFVocab:enables FunctionalArchitectureService
]

instance SimulationEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink simulation engine enabling dynamic behavioral modeling and system response analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralSimulationService
]

instance ArchitectureDescriptionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AADL-based architecture description language enabling standardized component-connector modeling for physical system layers."
    DTDFVocab:enables ArchitectureValidationService
]

instance DataAnalysisEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data processing pipeline enabling aggregation, abstraction, and validation of contextual data traces for analytics."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalysisService
]

instance ControlSystemEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Embedded control system middleware enabling closed-loop feedback between DT-generated insights and physical system parameters."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlFeedbackService
]

instance PerformanceAnalysisEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Performance benchmarking framework enabling comparison of real-time system states against historical baselines."
    DTDFVocab:enables PerformanceOptimizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance FunctionalArchitectureService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing SysML-based functional architecture visualization and validation for engineering teams."
    DTDFVocab:provides FunctionalArchitectureInsight
]

instance BehavioralSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling dynamic system behavior simulation using Simulink models for failure scenario testing."
    DTDFVocab:provides BehavioralSimulationInsight
]

instance ArchitectureValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service validating AADL-based component-connector architectures against system requirements and interfaces."
    DTDFVocab:provides ArchitectureValidationInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service generating predictive maintenance recommendations based on historical operational data analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveMaintenanceAction
]

instance ControlFeedbackService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing closed-loop control by adjusting physical system parameters based on DT control feedback."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlAdjustmentAction
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service identifying performance bottlenecks and proposing optimization strategies for system components."
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceOptimizationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FunctionalArchitectureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual and analytical insights into system functional architecture enabling requirements validation and design verification."
]

instance BehavioralSimulationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulated system behavior insights including failure scenarios and operational stress responses for risk assessment."
]

instance ArchitectureValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results comparing AADL-based architecture against system requirements and interface specifications."
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated maintenance scheduling recommendations based on predictive analytics of operational data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time parameter adjustments to physical system components based on DT control feedback."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Performance benchmarking results identifying optimization opportunities and efficiency improvements for system components."
]

// C1: Environment
instance IndustrialSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Heterogeneous industrial system environment encompassing manufacturing and cyber-physical components."
    base:contains OperationalLogSensor,
                 EnvironmentalParameterSensor,
                 MachineStateSensor,
                 ContextualDataSensor,
                 SysMLModelingEnabler,
                 SimulationEngineEnabler,
                 ArchitectureDescriptionEnabler,
                 DataAnalysisEnabler,
                 ControlSystemEnabler,
                 PerformanceAnalysisEnabler
]

// C5: Data Storage
instance ContextualDataStorage : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated storage of contextual data traces supporting multi-tool integration and abstraction across abstraction layers."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ContextualDataTracesTransmitted,
                 OperationalLogDataTransmitted,
                 EnvironmentalParameterDataTransmitted,
                 MachineStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAnalysisEnabler,
                 PerformanceAnalysisEnabler
]

// C7: Data Processing
instance DataAbstractionPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ETL pipeline enabling data filtering, normalization, and outlier detection for consistent contextual data representation."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalysisService,
                 PerformanceOptimizationService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in Digital Twin systems involves **bidirectional control flows** where the digital model actively influences or commands physical systems, though the provided documents do not explicitly detail this interaction. However, the context implies that:

1. **Command Types**: Digital Twins may generate **control commands** (e.g., adjustment signals, operational directives, or reconfiguration instructions) based on simulated or predictive analyses. These commands could include **parameter tuning, fault mitigation actions, or adaptive scheduling**.

2. **Control Message Formats**: While not specified, such commands would likely adhere to **standardized industrial protocols** (e.g., **OPC UA for real-time control, Modbus for legacy systems, or proprietary APIs** for custom implementations).

3. **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**: The document highlights the need for **active components** in DTs to process data, implying that **security and validation protocols** (e.g., **digital signatures, role-based access control, or message integrity checks**) would be critical to ensure command legitimacy and prevent unauthorized modifications.

4. **Feedback Systems**: The document does not describe feedback mechanisms explicitly, but the **Digital Shadow Reference Model** [32] distinguishes DTs from shadows by their ability to **push data back to physical systems**, suggesting closed-loop control via **real-time monitoring and adaptive feedback** (e.g., using **PID controllers, reinforcement learning, or model predictive control**).

5. **Conflict Resolution**: For concurrent commands or conflicting states, the document implies reliance on **consensus algorithms** or **priority-based resolution** (e.g., **time-sensitive networking (TSN) protocols** for deterministic control).

6. **Command Execution Confirmation**: While not detailed, confirmation mechanisms would likely involve **acknowledgment messages** (e.g., **ACK/NACK protocols**) or **state verification** via bidirectional data flows to ensure command execution integrity.

*Note: Specific technical details (e.g., latency thresholds, protocol stacks) are absent in the provided documents.*
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents emphasizes **synchronization between physical and digital components** through **one-way data flows** (as per the Digital Shadow Reference Model by Kritzinger et al. [32]), where data is aggregated and abstracted from physical objects in real-time or near-real-time for specific purposes. While explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, ms, or latency requirements) are not detailed, the context implies **temporal granularity** must align with the operational needs of the system, often requiring **low-latency processing** to enable timely decision-making. The documents reference **heterogeneous modeling tools** (e.g., SysML, Simulink, AADL) that may operate at varying time scales for functional, logical, and physical layers, necessitating **coordinated temporal abstraction** across these layers. No specific time-scale metrics (e.g., microsecond-level precision, millisecond updates) are provided, but the integration of **simulation tools** (e.g., Simulink) suggests **discrete-event or continuous-time modeling** with configurable time steps for behavioral analysis."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s **orchestration and coordination** is framed within **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, leveraging **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like AADL, MontiArc, or SysML IBDs to define **component composition, interfaces, and interconnections**. The **MontiArc Automaton** and **SysML state machines** (v1.7) provide **behavioral coordination** at the logical layer, while **simulation tools** (e.g., Simulink) and **general-purpose languages** (C++, Java) handle detailed behavioral execution. The **SMArDT methodology** [22] implies **adaptive orchestration** for dynamic system reconfiguration. **Resource management** is implied through **tool-specific frameworks** (e.g., Simulink’s real-time execution environments) and **heterogeneous integration**, though no explicit **resource allocation algorithms** (e.g., scheduling policies) are described. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** [32] further suggests **active components** (e.g., interpreters or processors) within the DT to enable **bidirectional data flow** (unlike passive shadows), requiring **system-wide coordination** to manage consistency between physical and digital states."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system addresses **model fidelity** through **multi-layered modeling** (e.g., SysML for architecture, Simulink for behavior, and general-purpose code for physics), ensuring alignment with **requirements, functional, logical, and technical layers**. Validation and verification (V&V) methods are implied via **model-based systems engineering (MBSE) methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) and **simulation tools** (e.g., Simulink) to detect discrepancies between digital and physical states. Uncertainty quantification arises from **sensor noise, model approximations, and stakeholder interpretation gaps**, though explicit **error detection/correction mechanisms** (e.g., Kalman filters, anomaly detection) or **quality assurance processes** (e.g., ISO/IEC standards) are not specified. The system prioritizes **contextual data aggregation** to mitigate uncertainty, but formal **fidelity metrics** or **uncertainty propagation frameworks** are not documented."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** for Digital Twin systems is not explicitly detailed in the provided documents, but the context implies the following:

1. **Communication Protocols**: Heterogeneous protocols are used for **data aggregation and abstraction**, including **MQTT (for IoT), OPC UA (for industrial interoperability), and RESTful APIs (for cloud integration)**. The document references **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **AADL or MontiArc**, which may underpin **component-based communication frameworks** (e.g., **DDS, ROS for robotics, or custom middleware**).

2. **Network Topology and Architecture**: The infrastructure likely follows a **hybrid topology** combining:
   - **Edge computing nodes** (for low-latency processing near physical assets),
   - **Cloud platforms** (for scalable analytics and storage),
   - **On-premise servers** (for legacy system integration).
   The document mentions **model-based systems engineering (MBSE)** frameworks (e.g., **SysML, SPES**) that standardize **component-connector models**, implying a **service-oriented architecture (SOA)** or **microservices-based deployment**.

3. **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**: Not specified, but the need for **real-time or near-real-time** data flows (e.g., for control systems) suggests **low-latency requirements** (e.g., **<100ms for critical applications**). The document implies **adaptive sampling** based on abstraction levels, which may influence bandwidth usage.

4. **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**: While not detailed, the **Digital Shadow Reference Model** [32] emphasizes **passive data collection**, suggesting redundancy via **replicated data streams** or **failover mechanisms** (e.g., **kubernetes-based orchestration** for cloud-edge deployments).

5. **Security Protocols**: The document does not specify security protocols, but given the context of **industrial systems**, protocols like **TLS for encryption, PKI for authentication, or IEC 62443 for cybersecurity** would likely be employed to protect data integrity and confidentiality.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **deployment infrastructure** for Digital Twin systems is inferred from the document’s discussion of **model-based systems engineering (MBSE)** and **heterogeneous tool integration**. Key aspects include:

1. **Hosting Platforms**: Deployment may occur across:
   - **Cloud platforms** (e.g., **AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud**) for scalable storage and compute,
   - **Edge devices** (e.g., **FPGAs, gateways, or industrial PCs**) for low-latency processing,
   - **On-premise servers** (e.g., **private data centers**) for legacy system integration or compliance requirements.

2. **Computational and Storage Requirements**: The document implies **high computational demands** due to:
   - **Simulation tools** (e.g., **MathWorks Simulink, ANSYS Fluent**),
   - **Model execution** (e.g., **finite element analysis, system dynamics simulations**),
   - **Data aggregation** (e.g., **time-series databases like InfluxDB or PostgreSQL**).
   Storage requirements would scale with **historical data retention policies** (e.g., **weeks/months for analytics, years for compliance**).

3. **Scalability Mechanisms**: The document references **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., **RFLP, OOSEM**) that modularize system descriptions, enabling **horizontal scaling** via:
   - **Containerization** (e.g., **Docker, Kubernetes**) for microservices,
   - **Virtualization** (e.g., **VMware, OpenStack**) for resource isolation,
   - **Dynamic resource allocation** based on workload (e.g., **auto-scaling in cloud environments**).

4. **Deployment Models Supported**: Likely includes:
   - **Cloud-native deployments** (e.g., **serverless functions for event-driven processing**),
   - **Hybrid cloud-edge models** (e.g., **data preprocessing at the edge, analytics in the cloud**),
   - **On-premise deployments** for air-gapped or high-security environments.

5. **Availability/Reliability Assurance**: While not detailed, mechanisms would include:
   - **Redundant data replication** (e.g., **multi-AZ deployments in cloud**),
   - **Disaster recovery plans** (e.g., **snapshots, backup protocols**),
   - **High-availability architectures** (e.g., **load balancers, failover clusters**).
   The document’s emphasis on **MBSE integration** suggests **version-controlled model repositories** (e.g., **Git-based workflows**) to ensure consistency across deployments.
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration in the described Digital Twin system is facilitated through **heterogeneous data exchange protocols** and **interoperability standards** to connect with external systems. The documents reference **MBSE tools** (e.g., SysML, Simulink) and **general-purpose languages** (C++, Java) as integration points, implying reliance on **standardized interfaces** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, or REST APIs) for cross-system communication, though these are not explicitly named. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** [32] highlights **one-way data flows** to external systems (e.g., IoT devices, enterprise systems) for aggregation and abstraction, suggesting **dependency on external data sources** for contextual enrichment. The **RFLP methodology** [18], [19] and **OOSEM** [20] emphasize **multi-perspective integration**, requiring **adapters or middleware** to reconcile disparate stakeholder needs. No specific **integration patterns** (e.g., microservices, event-driven architectures) or **enterprise system dependencies** (e.g., ERP, MES) are detailed beyond the implied need for **seamless data exchange** across tools and notations."
]

    base:desc "
    The document references **industry standards and methodologies** indirectly relevant to Digital Twin standardization:

    - **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) Standards**:
      - **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)** [17] is used for architecture description and behavior modeling, aligning with DT representation standards.
      - **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)**: **ACME**, **AADL**, **MontiArc**, and **SysML IBDs** provide structured frameworks for DT component and interface standardization.

    - **Interoperability**:
      The document highlights the need for **heterogeneous tool integration** (e.g., combining SysML, Simulink, and general-purpose code), suggesting adherence to **interoperability standards** (e.g., **ISO/IEC 42010** for architecture description, **OMG’s UML** for modeling).
      While not explicitly named, **open standards** (e.g., **OPC UA**, **ROS**) are implied for DT data exchange.

    - **Certification/Compliance Frameworks**:
      No explicit mention of **certification requirements** or **compliance frameworks** (e.g., ISO 23247 for DTs) is provided.

    - **Lack of Explicit DT Standards**:
      The document does not cite **Digital Twin-specific standards** (e.g., **IEEE P2414**, **ISO/IEC 23247**) or **certification bodies** (e.g., IEC, ANSI).

    **Summary**: The document aligns with **MBSE and ADL standards** but does not detail **DT-specific standardization**, **certification**, or **interoperability protocols** beyond tool integration.
  "
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot incorporates multiple **safety-critical mechanisms** and **cybersecurity protocols** to ensure operational integrity and threat mitigation. The **safety cell** restricts the maximum working range of the first vertical axis to [-90°, 90°], enforcing positional constraints via **hardware-based physical barriers** and **software-enforced limits** within the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller. The controller supports **fail-safe mechanisms** by allowing independent axis control with configurable inputs (position, speed, or acceleration) and outputs (target/actual position, derivatives, and motor current), enabling real-time monitoring of **safety-critical parameters** such as torque (τ_M, τ_G, τ_out) and positional uncertainty.

**Threat mitigation** is implicitly addressed through the **dual-encoder sensor arrangement** (motor encoder + secondary encoder post-gearbox), which reduces backlash and positional uncertainty by cross-verifying encoder data. This design mitigates **sensor-based deception attacks** by detecting inconsistencies in drive train elasticity/friction. However, explicit **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., authentication systems, access control, or risk assessment frameworks) are not documented in the provided context.

**Risk assessment** is partially implied via the **specified operational limits** (e.g., maximum speed |q̇_max| = 75°/s, positional accuracy ±0.1 mm, repeatability ±0.06 mm), which align with **safety-critical system considerations** for subtractive manufacturing (e.g., milling). The **gear transmission and cycloidal gear** design further enhances robustness against mechanical failures, though **software-based fail-safes** (e.g., emergency stop protocols) are not detailed.

**Note:** While the documents emphasize **physical safety** (e.g., safety cell, encoder redundancy) and **operational constraints**, **explicit data governance, privacy measures, or cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., encryption, role-based access control) are absent.
"
]
